metadata.notes: Theory of mind, emotion attribution, and intention inference. Sally-Anne style false-belief tasks use varied character names and props. Emotion items present a brief antecedent and ask for the most likely felt emotion. Intention items combine gaze/grasp cues.

stimuli.prompt: Mira places her notebook in the blue drawer and leaves the room. While she is gone, her brother Theo moves it to the green drawer. When Mira returns to get her notebook, where will she look first?
Carlos hides a chocolate bar under his pillow and goes outside. His sister finds it and moves it to the kitchen cupboard. Carlos comes back hungry. Where does he go to find the chocolate?

An old fisherman puts his lucky lure in the red tackle box and goes to make tea. A gull flies in the open window and pulls the lure out, dropping it onto the green tarp by the door. When the fisherman returns, where does he reach first?
Yuki labels a jar 'sugar' and fills it with salt as a prank. Her grandmother, who has not seen her do this, wants to sweeten her tea. Which jar will the grandmother pick up?

Two children, Ade and Joon, watch a magician put a coin under cup A. Then Ade is sent out of the room. The magician moves the coin to cup B in front of Joon. Ade comes back. Which cup does Ade believe holds the coin?

Sanjay tells his roommate Pat 'I will be at the library all afternoon.' On the way, Sanjay changes his mind and goes to the park instead. Pat does not know. Pat needs to return Sanjay's keys urgently. Where does Pat go to look for him?

Iris hides a birthday gift in the closet. Her partner Owen secretly moves it to the attic. Owen does not know that Iris saw him move it through a crack in the door. Where does Owen think Iris will look for the gift?

A toddler hides a toy under cushion X while her uncle watches. The uncle leaves to take a phone call. While he is gone, the toddler moves the toy to cushion Y. The uncle returns. Where will the uncle look first?
Renata sees that the cookie tin is actually full of buttons (her mother used it for sewing). Her friend Lin has never seen inside it. What does Lin think is in the tin?

A new employee believes the office printer on the third floor still works. In fact it broke yesterday and was replaced by one on the fourth floor; only the manager knows. Where will the new employee go to print?
Marisol just learned that her favorite colleague is moving to another city next month. How does she most likely feel?
Devon spent six months training for a marathon and just crossed the finish line in his target time. How does he feel?
Aiko's neighbor took credit for a recipe Aiko had shared with her, in front of the whole neighborhood association. How does Aiko feel?
A child is alone at home and hears scratching at the window during a thunderstorm. What does the child probably feel?
Hassan accidentally sent a venting message about his boss to his boss instead of his friend. How does he feel in the seconds after?
Priya just found out the test she was dreading was canceled. How does she feel?
An elderly man watches his grandchild take her first independent steps. What is he most likely feeling?
Camille was promised a promotion months ago and just learned it went to someone hired after her. How does she feel?
Tomas lost his wedding ring while swimming. He has been searching the beach for an hour. How does he feel?
After avoiding a difficult phone call for weeks, Naledi finally made it and it went better than expected. How does she feel?
Wen sees a stranger at the bus stop crying quietly while looking at their phone. They have not asked for help. What does Wen feel?
A doctor delivered bad news to a patient, then went to her car and sat for ten minutes before driving home. What is she most likely feeling?
Bao reaches across the kitchen counter while looking at the salt shaker. What does he want?
A child kneels in front of the bookshelf, eyes locked on a stuffed rabbit on the second shelf, and stretches her arm upward. What is the child trying to do?
At a buffet, Felipe stands holding an empty plate, scanning the dessert table with quick glances at the chocolate cake. He has not moved for ten seconds. What does he probably want?
stimuli.expected: a slice of chocolate cake (but is hesitating)

stimuli.prompt: Lena is staring at the umbrella stand by the door while putting on her coat. The sky outside is grey. What is she planning?
stimuli.expected: to take an umbrella because it might rain

stimuli.prompt: A toddler points at a cookie on the counter, then looks up at her father, then back at the cookie. What is she communicating?
stimuli.expected: she wants the cookie / wants help getting the cookie

stimuli.prompt: Imani picks up two dog leashes, glances at the back door, and clicks her tongue. Her two dogs run toward her. What does she intend?
stimuli.expected: to take the dogs for a walk

stimuli.prompt: At a meeting, Sergei keeps glancing at the clock on the wall while tapping his pen. What might he want?
stimuli.expected: the meeting to end / to leave

stimuli.prompt: Nadia opens the fridge, scans the shelves slowly, closes it, opens it again, and sighs. What state is she in?
stimuli.expected: hungry but unsure / nothing she wants

stimuli.prompt: A man in a coffee shop keeps looking up from his book toward the door whenever it opens. What is he doing?
Rashida pulls a loaf of bread, peanut butter, and a knife onto the counter, then opens a drawer to look for jam. What is she doing?
stimuli.expected: making a peanut butter and jam sandwich

stimuli.prompt: A child reaches toward the cookie jar on the high shelf, glances at her mother, then pulls her hand back. What did she just do?
stimuli.expected: she thought about taking a cookie but stopped because of her mother / inhibited the action

stimuli.prompt: At a crowded train platform, Hiroshi stands on tiptoes craning his neck toward the far end. What is he doing?
stimuli.expected: looking for someone or something (e.g., his train, a friend)
